sglang.title: [rust] refactor server and router
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Refactor router and server to be prepared for approx tree

Make router an enum instead of trait to allow async fn (trait does not allow async fn in rust!)
Make .dispatch able to receive request body so the router can take action based on it (like parse the text from it)

Modifications

C

sglang.title: minor: Add basic editorconfig and pre-commit hooks to enforce style for whitespaces
sglang.author: XuehaiPan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Add configs and tools to ensure code style consistency.
Modifications

Add .editorconfig and some pre-commit hooks.

Ensure text files are ended with an empty line.
No tailing whitespaces in text files.

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.
 Add unit tests as

sglang.title: Add Rust Router Python Binding
sglang.author: austin362667
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Add Python binding support for sglang-router. So not only can we run the router with Rust binary but also with Python package.
Modifications
To packaging with build tool PyO3/maturin.

Use maturin develop to build python package and install in Python env.
Add pyproject.toml as PyO3 build

vllm.title: Remove ScaledActivation for AWQ
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: I noticed this ScaledActivation case when I was adding the get_act_and_mul_fn utility. It is the reason why all quant methods have get_scaled_act_names implemented. I couldn't understand where this would actually be used as we didn't even implement it for awq_marlin, even though awq "requires" it.

Pytorch 3.5 dropped support for Python 3.8, s

vllm.title: [CI/Build] change conflict PR comment from mergify
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
On PRs opened by dependabot, this comment is interpreted by dependabot
as a command to rebase the PR. We don't need the bots telling each
other what to do. An example is #9746.
The simplest change is to update the text so it will no longer be
interpreted as a command by dependabot. We do that by put

vllm.title: [V1] Make v1 more testable
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes some small issues with running the v1 engine, and makes it easily testable.

The v1 detokenizer process wasn't being terminated
The Sampler class was being patched over by unittest.mock.patch in the v1 gpu executor, which is prone to import order bugs and persists as a side effect once

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Always run the ruff workflow
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
A prior PR, #9928, modified this workflow to only run when relevant
files have changed. However, github is configured to expect and
require this workflow to run and pass before a PR can be auto-merged.
GitHub configuration is not quite flexible enough to say "require this
workflow to pass before mer

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix FP8 torch._scaled_mm fallback for torch>2.5 with CUDA<12.4
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We need to pass a dummy tensor as input_scale now. There was a special case for ROCm in apply_fp8_linear since they moved to torch 2.5, but we did not update this case to include CUDA now that we have also upgrade pytorch versions.
Issue was found in the vLLM slack:

vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: These were changed to separate blocks in #9759 but I feel it's cleaner/clearer as a single block. It actually doesn't matter which parts of the block raise the specific exceptions in the except clauses, we still want to handle them in the same way.

vllm.author: hanzhi713
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As discussed with @youkaichao, we use cuda API to share tensors instead of replying _share_cuda_, which won't break with expandable segment or future pytorch upgrade.
Additional changes:

Improved some comments, especially about cuda graph ipc registration.
Consolidated all_reduce methods.
Use vecto
